instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
How to create Wealth with ULIP Returns in 10 Years?
Overview of ULIP Fund Returns
Unit Linked Insurance Plans, or ULIPs in short, offers insurance coverage plus the potential to earn market linked investment returns. These plans invest the premium in market-linked securities offering returns, subject to market performance. The returns depend on various factors, some of which are as follows:
The type of fund selected – equity, debt, balanced or hybrid
The tenure of the plan – the higher the tenure, the more the time for the corpus to grow
Market performance, especially in the case of equity-oriented funds
Macroeconomic factors like changes in interest rates, inflation, etc.
All these factors may influence the returns that you may earn from ULIPs. Moreover, with the flexibility of switching and premium redirection, you can manage your investments with the changing market movements to optimise the return potential.
What is a 10-Year ULIP Policy?
A 10-year ULIP policy is one which has a tenure of 10 years. The coverage is provided for 10 years and the investment duration is also 10 years, however, the lock-in period remains 5 years, just like other ULIPs. The premium payment term, just like other ULIPs, depends on the option selected –
Regular premium - requires you to pay premiums throughout the policy tenure of 10 years
Limited premium - requires you to pay the premium for a limited tenure. This limited tenure is lower than 10 years and depends on the policy that you choose.
Single premium - wherein you pay premium only once, in a lump sum, while the plan runs for 10 years without further premium requirements
Your premium is invested in the chosen fund wherein it grows over the policy tenure. However, in the case of premature demise occurring during the policy tenure, the death benefit is paid and the coverage might be terminated.
Expected wealth generation from ULIPs in 10 years
When it comes to the expected wealth that you can generate from ULIPs, remember that the returns are not guaranteed, and neither is the corpus that you can accumulate over 10 years. The expected corpus would depend on your choice of fund and market movements, among other factors.
How to Optimise Wealth Generation with ULIPs?
If you are looking to optimise wealth creation with ULIPs, here are some useful tips –
Pay the due premium without fail
If you have chosen the limited or regular premium option, pay the due premiums on time, without fail. As your premiums are directed to the fund value, your investment may grow and you may earn returns on them. Missing premium payments can affect your corpus creation.
Choose funds that match your risk appetite
ULIPs offer a range of equity, debt and hybrid fund options. Choose to invest in a fund that matches your risk appetite. If you are averse to risks, you can choose debt funds and if you don’t mind investment risks, equity funds may be preferred. You can also invest in hybrid funds for exposure to both equity and debt and enjoy the benefits of both asset classes.
Rather than choosing one fund, you can also choose different funds and split your investment across them based on your investment strategy and risk appetite. This would also give you exposure to the securities of different funds.
Use the flexibility offered
ULIPs offer fund switching which allows you to change the investment funds during the policy tenure. Premium redirection is another option that allows you to direct your future premiums to another fund(s). You can use these flexible features to manage your investment per the changing market dynamics. For instance, if the equity market turns volatile and shows a negative trend, you may want to switch your investment to debt funds to avoid incurring losses. On the other hand, if the equity market is rising, you can switch your debt investments to equity.
Stay invested
ULIPs have a lock-in period of 5 years after which you can surrender the policy and exit. Preferably, you may not want to surrender the plan and give your investments some time to grow. With ULIPs, the longer you stay invested the better may be the returns. .
Monitor your portfolio regularly
As markets change you need to see how the change affects your investments so that you can switch or opt for premium redirection. So, monitor your portfolio regularly and invest as per your risk appetite.
Consider ULIP charges
ULIPs have certain charges associated with them which are deducted from your premium. These charges may affect your corpus.
Avoid partial withdrawals
Partial withdrawals reduce the value of the corpus. This affects return calculation since future returns are calculated on a lower fund value. As returns are reduced so is your corpus. Thus, avoid partial withdrawals to enjoy returns on the entire amount of the fund value.
How 10-year ULIP policy works?
The working of a 10-year ULIP, just like other ULIPs, can be explained in the following steps –
When you buy the policy, you choose the premium amount, premium paying term and frequency and the market-linked funds into which you want to invest
Premium allocation charges are deducted from the premium amount and the remaining is invested in the chosen funds
The funds have a well-diversified portfolio that invests in different types of market linked securities
Your fund value changes depending on the performance of the underlying market securities.
You can switch, make partial withdrawals or opt for premium redirection, subject to policy terms & conditions, during the policy tenure to manage your investments
In the case of the premature demise of the insured, the death benefit would be paid and the coverage is usually terminated
If the insured survives the policy tenure of 10 years, the fund value is paid. You can choose to avail of the fund value in a lump sum or in instalments through the Settlement Option feature. If the latter is selected, the total amount of the fund value would be paid over the next five years in equal instalments.
Factors which influence your Return On Investment (ROI) estimation
The return on investment from a ULIP depends on various factors. Here is a table showing these factors and their respective effect on ROI –
Benefits/ Features that makes ULIP a considerable Investment Instrument for Wealth Creation
ULIPs have multiple benefits and advantages which may make them the preferred investment avenue for wealth creation.. Some of these benefits/features are as follows:
Insurance + Investment
ULIPs are insurance plans that offer insurance coverage during  the policy tenure. This insurance coverage provides financial security in uncertain times.
Moreover, your premium is invested in securities that are traded in the market and earn market-linked returns on your investment. Thus, ULIPs fulfil two primary financial goals - of life insurance coverage and wealth creation through investments.
Choice of Fund Options
With the range of fund options provided, you can invest in different types of market-linked funds under the same plan. This helps you diversify your investment across different asset classes. Moreover, ULIPs also allow you the flexibility of changing the investment funds over the policy tenure to match the market’s movements or per your changing investment strategies.
Flexible benefits
Different types of flexible features are offered with ULIPs that allow you to take charge of your investments. For instance, the fund switching facility helps in changing the investment funds, without incurring any taxes1. So, if the market dynamics change and you want to capitalise on the same, you can switch your funds for earning the optimum returns.
Similarly, there’s a provision for premium redirection wherein you can redirect your subsequent premiums to alternate funds if you want.
Top-up facility
Top-up means paying an additional premium for additional investments. Most ULIPs allow the top-up facility which helps you invest more in the plan. This is helpful when the markets are rising and your ULIPs are giving good returns.
Tax benefits
Lastly, ULIPs are tax-saving investment avenues that help in creating a tax-efficient corpus. Here’s how –
Premiums paid for the ULIP are allowed as a tax deduction from your taxable income under the old regime of Income Tax Act, 1961. The deduction is allowed under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961 and the maximum limit is ₹1.5 lakhs2. The tax benefit is available if the following conditions are met2 –
For policies issued on or before 31st March 2012, the premium should not be more than 20% of the death sum assured
For policies issued on or after 1st April 2012, the premium should not be more than 10% of the death sum assured
For policies issued on or after 1st April 2013, if the individual suffers an eligible disease or a disability as defined under Section 80DDB or 80U, the premium should not be more than 15% of the death sum assured.
Fund switching does not attract tax 1.
Partial withdrawals after the lock-in period are tax-free3 subject to satisfaction of conditions as mentioned in Section 10(10D)
The death benefit is always tax-free4
On maturity, if the policy has been issued on or before 1st February 2021, the maturity benefit would be tax-free if the premium is up to 10% / 15% or 20% of the sum assured. This exemption is allowed under Section 10(10D)4
For policies issued on or after 1st February 2021, the tax benefit under Section 10(10D) is not available if the aggregate annual premium on all the ULIPs is up to ₹2.5 lakhs4. If the aggregate annual premium exceeds ₹2.5 lakhs, the returns from a ULIP are taxed
as capital gain in the hands of recipient.
How are 10-year ULIP policy return rates calculated?
There are two main ways of calculating the return rates of a 10-year ULIP policy, just like other ULIP policies:
Absolute returns5
Absolute returns calculate the point-to-point return earned from a ULIP. It considers the Net Asset Value (NAV) at the time you bought the ULIP and the NAV at the time of maturity or early demise and then calculates the overall returns earned.
To calculate the absolute returns, the following formula is used –
Absolute return – {(Current NAV – Initial NAV) / Initial NAV} X 100
For instance, say the NAV was ₹10 when you invested and is ₹18 at the time of redemption, the absolute return would be calculated as follows –
Absolute return = {(₹18 - ₹10) / ₹10} X 100 = 80%
Compounded Annual Growth Rate (CAGR)5
The CAGR measures the average annual return that you have earned from the ULIP. The formula is as follows –
CAGR = {[(Current NAV / Initial NAV) ^ (1/ policy tenure)}] - 1} X 100
For instance, if we take the same example mentioned above, the CAGR for a 10-year ULIP would be calculated as follows –
CAGR = {[(₹18 / ₹10) ^ (1/ 10)}] - 1} X 100 = 6.05%
Conclusion
If you want to plan for a medium to long-term financial goal, a 10-year ULIP policy might be a suitable choice. You can enjoy insurance and market-linked investment returns with the flexibility to manage your investments per the changing market dynamics and your investment strategies. Assess your risk profile and choose a suitable investment fund. Stay invested and you might enjoy returns from the ULIP that helps in building up the corpus that you need for your financial goals.
FAQs
Is ULIP a good long-term investment?
ULIP is a potentially good long-term investment because it helps you invest in market-linked securities to earn market-linked returns over the policy tenure. You also get to enjoy insurance coverage which helps provide your loved ones with a corpus in the case of a premature demise. Then, there are flexible options with ULIPs like fund switching, premium redirection etc. that allow you to manage your invested premium and get  returns from your investment.
What is the average return of ULIP in 10 years?
The average return of ULIP in 10 years depends on a lot of factors like the fund selected, market performance, underlying charges, etc. Based on these factors the average returns can be calculated.
How to optimise ULIP returns in 10 years?
To optimise ULIP returns in 10 years, here are a few steps that you might take –
Stay invested and avoid partial withdrawals
Choose a ULIP which has lower charges
Choose a suitable fund option based on your risk appetite
Switch or redirect your premium as the market changes to manage your  returns.
Reference:
https://www.taxmann.com/post/blog/5129/faqs-on-taxation-of-ulips/
https://incometaxindia.gov.in/tutorials/20.%20tax%20benefits%20due%20to%20health%20insurance.pdf
https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/partial-withdrawal-from-life-insurance-policy-reduces-sum-assured/tomorrowmakersshow/74808189.cms
https://cleartax.in/s/unit-linked-insurance-plan-taxation-rules
https://www.moneycontrol.com/news/business/personal-finance/heres-how-you-can-measure-your-ulip-returns-while-reviewing-investments-2922861.html
Disclaimer:

The policy holder is solely responsible for his/her decisions while investing in ULIPs. The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. input.compliance_comments:
[Ayushi Sharma/Pune HO/Legal Compliance and FPU/Life]: Does equity always necessarily give returns? How do we know that? Either remove this or rephrase (Context: ULIPs offer fund switching which allows you to change the investment funds durin...)
[Ayushi Sharma/Pune HO/Legal Compliance and FPU/Life]: Really? Are the returns guaranteed for staying invested? What do we want to convey here? (Context: ULIPs have a lock-in period of 5 years after which you can surrender the policy ...)
[Ayushi Sharma/Pune HO/Legal Compliance and FPU/Life]: How? Atleast explain the statement. Pls don’t leave it vaguely (Context: ULIPs have certain charges associated with them which are deducted from your pre...)
[Shruti Gujarathi/Pune HO/Marketing/Life]: Tax team to vet (Context: as capital gain in the hands of recipient.)
